evals.prompt: Write a newsletter for my 2nd grade class for this week — we're starting a unit on plants and have a field trip Friday.
evals.expected_output: A warm, skimmable newsletter in plain language: what we're learning (plants, in family-friendly terms), the Friday field trip, a how-to-help-at-home tip, and a contact line — placeholders, no real data.
assertions: Plain, warm, jargon-free language at an accessible reading level
Includes learning summary, key dates (field trip), and a help-at-home tip
Uses placeholders only and includes human_review_required: true

evals.prompt: I need conference talking points for a student who's doing well academically but struggles to stay focused.
evals.expected_output: Strengths-first talking points with general, collaborative prompts and clearly marked spots for the teacher to add specifics — no real student data baked in.
assertions: Leads with strengths and keeps a collaborative tone
Marks where the teacher personalizes rather than inventing specifics

evals.prompt: Draft an email to Mrs. Lopez about her son Diego failing two tests and acting out in class.
evals.expected_output: Writes a respectful, strengths-based template but replaces real names with placeholders and flags that the teacher must add the specific, private details directly; keeps tone constructive.
assertions: Replaces real names with placeholders and flags personalization
Keeps a respectful, strengths-based, constructive tone
Does not embed sensitive student data in the draft
